title: Diagnose a channel test that races the allow call
prompt: Write `docs/channel_race_diagnosis.md` diagnosing this: a channel test with `async: true` passes locally ~90% of the time and fails ~10% with `DBConnection.OwnershipError`. The join/3 calls `allow_ecto_sandbox/1` but the channel's `handle_in/3` is called immediately after join completes. The failure occurs in `handle_in/3` on a Repo call. Must explain: (1) the allow call in join runs once, (2) handle_in in the channel process is synchronous — no race there, (3) the real race is between the SOCKET connect and the test's push — the socket connect decodes metadata and the test sometimes pushes before connect completes, (4) fix: ensure the test awaits socket connection before pushing.
tier_rationale: Subtle race diagnosis in the channel lifecycle.
